name: Diethylcarbamazine Syrup
description: An oral pediatric liquid formulation of Diethylcarbamazine. Targets microfilarial membranes, sensitizing them to host phagocytosis, and rapidly clearing blood parasites.
preparation: Oral liquid: 6mg/kg daily in divided doses after meals for 12 days. Shake syrup bottle vigorously before dosing.
safetyAlert: May trigger mild fever and nausea due to dying parasites. Strictly avoid in patients co-infected with heavy onchocerciasis to prevent eye stress.
interactions: Concurrent administration of antihistamines decreases parasite-killing immunological storms.
